zh.lifeSummary: 當William Selby於1475年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋時，他的父親Walter Selby39歲，母親Alice Brandling27歲。他和Eleanor Hebborne De Gray至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1565年6月在英國英格蘭越可爾約克享年90歲。

nameMeanings.name: Selby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire) and Scottish: habitational name from Selby (Yorkshire). The placename may derive from Old English sele ‘willow copse’, sometimes alternating with salh ‘willow’, + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Shelby
meanings: English (Essex and Middlesex): probably a variant of   Selby  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Selby was born in 1475, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter Selby, was 39 and his mother, Alice Brandling, was 27. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Eleanor Hebborne De Gray. He died in June 1565, in York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 90.
